title: World regions in the SDG framework of the United Nations
caption:
World regions in the SDG framework of the United Nations

This choropleth world map displays the geographic groupings used in the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals framework. The legend defines eight distinct regions, each assigned a unique color to categorize countries. Europe and Northern America are shaded in blue, covering the entirety of the European continent, Russia, the United States, and Canada. Latin America and the Caribbean appear in dark red, encompassing Mexico, Central America, and South America. Sub-Saharan Africa is colored purple, occupying the majority of the African continent south of the Sahara. Northern Africa and Western Asia are shown in tan, stretching from Morocco across the Middle East. Central and Southern Asia are grouped in green, including India and surrounding nations. Eastern and South-Eastern Asia are colored orange, covering China, Japan, and the Indochinese peninsula. Australia and New Zealand form a distinct teal region, while the scattered island nations of Oceania are shaded in light blue. The map presents a clear partition of the globe where every country falls into exactly one of these eight defined regional blocks, with no overlaps or uncolored territories visible on the main landmasses.
